title: ESS Eclipse (ESN-EX-01)
content:
The Eclipse was an [[Eclipse-Class Dreadnought]] built by [[United Equus]] during the Great War. She served as the flagship of the [[Equus Star Navy]] for two years until her defection in 2438. Between 2438 and 2444, she was the largest ship fielded by the Continuation Forces, and served as their flagship and headquarters. Following the Hauxipec Accords, she was readmitted into the ESN; where after the tribunal of her CO, Admiral Nebula Bolt, she was relegated to the role of a frontier training vessel. In 2450, she would drift into the Great Mists and lose primary power, resulting in the eventual death of all hands, save for Admiral Bolt. Admiral Bolt survived due to his connection with the immensely powerful magical creature known as the Tantabus, the two eventually becoming symbiotic to one another.

In 2962, she was rediscovered by the ISS Hammer of Fire, a frontier scout ship of United Equus' successor state, the Solar Empire. The Hammer of Fire awoke Admiral Bolt (now going as "the Tantabus") from stasis, who, upon learning of the atrocities committed by the Solar Empire, killed all Equestrian crew members on the Hammer of Fire. Shortly thereafter, the Tantabus activated the Pegus War Fleet (colloquially referred to as the Nightmare Fleet), with the Eclipse becoming it's new flagship. She would take a pivotal role in the Battle of Equus, bringing an end to Empress Celestia's rule.

The Eclipse and the rest of the Nightmare Fleet would go into hiding during most of the reign of Princess Luna, returning to service as the Tantabus' headquarters, a role it would serve for the rest of it's history.

Development and Construction

The Eclipse represented a drastic departure from previous Equestrian ship design: it's imposing figure was meant to act as a tool of psychological warfare, in addition to the massive traditional armament it carried. Primarily designed by Admiral Nebula Bolt, a leading starship engineer before the war, she was intended to be a direct counter to the Apleen A-1 battlecruiser, although she also served as a test bed for highly experimental and costly technologies.

She would be the first of three ships ever outfitted with a Twinkle-Dixion E-R Bridge Generation Device (A temporary wormhole generator), and was capable of transporting entire an entire fleet at much greater speeds that typical FTL devices. The three superlasers it was equipped with were reverse engineered versions of the A-1's more powerful superlaser, although their destructive properties were still far beyond anything achieved by Equus beforehand. She would also be the first ship to carry large ion pulse devices, later honed by the Nightmare-class Battleship. The final important experimental technology she carried was the Arcane Grid Deflectors that would later become the standard for Equestrian ships even into the reign of Princess Luna.

First laid down in 2434, the Eclipse was a monumental effort on the part of United Equus, taking up the bulk of their shipbuilding capability, which was a major factor in the ESN fielding drastically fewer ships than it's allies for the first half of the war. The Equus Orbital Yards had to be physically expanded to be able to fit the the Eclipse in her berth. Production was momentarily halted after Admiral Bolt was rendered comatose in 2435, but quickly began again without him. He would awake shortly before it's completion in 2436.
